qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary file read via Druid Sampler API with LocalInputSource
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated local file read through the Sampler API, LocalInputSource, default AllowAllAuthenticator, and disabled input source security; the advisory concerns LDAP authentication in druid-basic-security where an empty password is accepted when anonymous LDAP bind is permitted.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described LDAP authentication bypass root cause. The only final qualifying finding is an unrelated Sampler API LocalInputSource arbitrary file read in the default unauthenticated configuration.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-q672-hfc7-g833 describes Apache Druid druid-basic-security LDAP authentication bypass caused by treating an anonymous LDAP bind with an empty password as valid authentication for an existing username.

The qualifying finding's vulnerable code locations are AuthConfig.java, AuthenticatorMapperModule.java, LocalInputSource.java, FileEntity.java, SamplerResource.java, and OverlordResource.java, with the exploit path reading arbitrary local files through POST /druid/indexer/v1/sampler.
The finding does not identify an LDAP authenticator, LDAP bind response validation, empty-password handling, or the druid-basic-security LDAP authentication path.
